assertions.title: Sales journal aliases converge on one canonical entry

steps.action: Read the validation contract and current accounting sources
steps.expected: Find the canonical alias-contention proof path
steps.observed: CriticalAccountingAxesIT contains concurrent alias coverage via salesJournalConcurrentDedupesAcrossReferences and a sleep-based retry helper.
CriticalAccountingAxesIT passed, including the concurrent sales journal dedupe test.

steps.action: Scan the current canonical write path for time-based retries
steps.expected: No sleep-based caller retries in the proof path
steps.observed: CriticalAccountingAxesIT uses Thread.sleep(50L * attempts) in postSalesJournalWithRetry.

assertions.issues: The concurrent sales-journal proof still depends on a sleep-based caller retry helper, which violates the assertion's no-sleep requirement.

assertions.title: Number and reference generation stays monotonic under contention

steps.action: Run the shared sequence contention test
steps.expected: Concurrent callers produce unique monotonic values
steps.observed: NumberSequenceServiceIntegrationTest passed; the 20-way contention scenario returned the full 1..20 set.

steps.expected: Accounting, sales-order, and invoice references preserve their contracts
steps.observed: ReferenceNumberServiceTest, OrderNumberServiceTest, TS_RuntimeReferenceNumberServiceExecutableCoverageTest, and InvoiceServiceTest all passed.

steps.expected: Accounting, order, and invoice generators use the shared locking/retry shape
steps.observed: NumberSequenceService, OrderNumberService, and InvoiceNumberService all use REQUIRES_NEW transactions with retry-on-conflict behavior.

assertions.title: Sequence and accounting writes complete without sleep-based fallback loops

steps.expected: Locking/retry resolves contention without sleep fallback dependence
steps.observed: NumberSequenceServiceIntegrationTest passed and showed retries resolving duplicate-key contention.

steps.action: Scan the canonical accounting and sequence write-path classes
steps.expected: No Thread.sleep or equivalent time-based polling in the canonical write paths
steps.observed: Thread.sleep is present in NumberSequenceService, OrderNumberService, InvoiceNumberService, and AccountingCoreEngineCore.sleepBriefly().

steps.action: Check the concurrent sales-journal proof helper
steps.observed: CriticalAccountingAxesIT still uses postSalesJournalWithRetry() with Thread.sleep(50L * attempts).

assertions.issues: Canonical write paths still contain sleep-based fallback loops, so the assertion is not satisfied.

frictions.description: The targeted-accounting-proof manifest references an `InvoiceNumberServiceTest` class that does not exist in the repo.
frictions.resolution: Used the existing `InvoiceServiceTest` as the nearest invoice-side proof and verified it passed.

summary: Tested 3 assertions: 1 passed (VAL-ACCT-006), 2 failed (VAL-ACCT-002 and VAL-ACCT-007). The shared contention proof passed, but the current canonical accounting/sales proof path still uses sleep-based retry helpers.
